long_description.overview: Pure semantic contracts for HoneyDrunk.OS Grid. Provides zero-dependency abstractions for Grid context (three-tier model: GridContext, NodeContext, OperationContext), Node lifecycle orchestration, strongly-typed identity primitives (CorrelationId, CausationId, NodeId, TenantId, etc.), telemetry interfaces, transport binder contracts, structured error handling (HoneyDrunkException, ErrorCode, IErrorClassifier), agent execution contracts, and hosting abstractions (INodeDescriptor, GridOptions, IModule).
long_description.why_it_exists: A clean dependency graph requires explicit contracts at the foundation. Everything builds on these interfaces. Safe for libraries and SDKs—no runtime weight.
long_description.primary_audience: All HoneyDrunk modules and services; library authors building Grid-aware components without pulling in implementations.
value_props: Zero-dependency interface definitions (only .NET BCL + Microsoft.Extensions.* abstractions)
Strongly-typed identity primitives with validation at construction time
Three-tier context model (Grid → Node → Operation) with correlation and causation tracking
Lifecycle hooks, health/readiness contributors, and lifecycle stage contracts
OpenTelemetry-ready telemetry interfaces and tag conventions
Structured error handling with classification and well-known error codes
Transport binder contracts for context propagation across boundaries
Agent execution context contracts for LLM-centric components
long_description.monetization_signal: Open source foundation; enterprise patterns emerge in implementations.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Stabilize identity registries as the DNS of the Grid. Finalize agent execution context contracts for AgentKit integration.
long_description.grid_relationship: Foundation for Vault, Transport, Data, Auth, Pulse, AgentKit, and all other Core modules. Every node depends on these contracts.
long_description.demo_path: Reference these interfaces when building new modules, implementing custom handlers, or creating Grid-aware libraries.
long_description.signal_quote: The contracts that connect the Grid.

long_description.overview: Provider-agnostic persistence contracts defining how nodes interact with durable storage without coupling to databases or ORMs.
long_description.why_it_exists: Prevents provider leakage and inconsistent persistence patterns across the Grid.
long_description.primary_audience: Service and node developers consuming persistence capabilities.
value_props: Clean separation between contracts and implementations
long_description.roadmap_focus: Harden tenancy and schema version contracts.
long_description.grid_relationship: Defines contracts implemented by Data providers and consumed by service nodes.
long_description.demo_path: Reference abstractions from a service and swap providers without code changes.

long_description.overview: Entity Framework Core implementation of HoneyDrunk.Data persistence contracts.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides a production-ready ORM provider while keeping EF Core isolated behind Data contracts.
long_description.primary_audience: Service developers using EF Core–backed persistence.
value_props: EF Core isolated from consuming nodes
long_description.roadmap_focus: Refine DbContext base patterns and modeling conventions.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements HoneyDrunk.Data contracts using Entity Framework Core.
long_description.demo_path: Persist entities through repositories without exposing DbContext.

long_description.overview: SQL Server–specific persistence adapter implementing HoneyDrunk.Data contracts with standardized schema and migration conventions.
long_description.why_it_exists: Most Grid deployments target SQL Server initially and need consistent, auditable persistence behavior.
long_description.primary_audience: Service owners deploying on SQL Server.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Drift detection and schema safety rails.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Data adapter slot for SQL Server deployments.
long_description.demo_path: Apply migrations and persist data using SQL Server-backed providers.

long_description.overview: Migrations and schema evolution tooling for HoneyDrunk.Data providers.
long_description.why_it_exists: Separates schema evolution concerns from runtime persistence to keep deployments safe and auditable.
long_description.primary_audience: Infrastructure maintainers and CI/CD pipelines.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Migration safety checks and drift reporting.
long_description.grid_relationship: Supports Data providers by managing schema lifecycle without being consumed at runtime.
long_description.demo_path: Generate and apply migrations as part of deployment pipelines.

long_description.overview: Testing utilities, fixtures, and helpers for HoneyDrunk.Data integration testing.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides reusable test infrastructure for integration tests against data providers without duplicating setup logic.
long_description.primary_audience: Developers writing integration tests for Data-backed services.
value_props: Consistent test database setup and teardown
Factory methods for common test scenarios
SQLite and in-memory provider support for fast tests
long_description.roadmap_focus: Expand fixture factories and deterministic seeding.
long_description.grid_relationship: Supports Data providers by providing test harnesses without runtime dependencies.
long_description.demo_path: Use fixtures to spin up test databases for integration tests.

long_description.overview: Provider-agnostic outbox contracts defining the storage and dispatch interfaces for transactional outbox pattern. Separates outbox concerns from both Data providers and Transport dispatch.
long_description.why_it_exists: Enables exactly-once message delivery by storing outbox entries within database transactions, while keeping outbox contracts independent of any specific persistence or transport provider.
long_description.primary_audience: Service developers implementing transactional messaging patterns.
value_props: Clean separation between outbox storage and dispatch
long_description.roadmap_focus: Expand outbox metadata and diagnostics contracts.
long_description.grid_relationship: Defines contracts implemented by Data.Outbox and consumed by Data.Outbox.Dispatcher.
long_description.demo_path: Reference outbox abstractions to store messages within a UnitOfWork transaction.

long_description.overview: Outbox storage implementation using EF Core. Stores outbox entries within the same database transaction as business operations, ensuring atomicity between state changes and message publication.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides the persistence side of the transactional outbox pattern, keeping message storage atomic with business state changes.
long_description.primary_audience: Service developers using EF Core who need exactly-once messaging.
value_props: Atomic message storage within EF Core transactions
Lease-based concurrency for safe multi-instance dispatch
long_description.roadmap_focus: Batch optimization and diagnostics hooks.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements Outbox.Abstractions contracts. Consumed by Outbox.Dispatcher for message delivery.
long_description.demo_path: Save a domain event within a UnitOfWork and observe it persisted in the outbox table.
long_description.signal_quote: Persist the intent, deliver the fact.

long_description.overview: Background dispatcher that polls the outbox table and publishes pending messages via HoneyDrunk.Transport. Uses lease-based concurrency control for safe multi-instance operation with configurable retry and backoff.
long_description.why_it_exists: Completes the outbox pattern by bridging persisted outbox entries to Transport for reliable delivery, decoupling dispatch timing from business transactions.
long_description.primary_audience: Service developers deploying transactional outbox with background processing.
value_props: Retry with backoff for failed dispatches
Transport-agnostic — works with any registered Transport provider
long_description.roadmap_focus: Batch dispatch optimization and Pulse telemetry hooks.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Outbox.Abstractions for storage access and HoneyDrunk.Transport for message publishing.
long_description.demo_path: Enable outbox dispatcher → observe background publishing of transactional messages via Transport.

long_description.overview: Core authentication and authorization runtime for HoneyDrunk Grid nodes. Provides JWT Bearer token validation via Vault-backed signing keys, policy-based authorization evaluation, and lifecycle integration with Kernel. This is a Grid node component—not a standalone library—requiring Kernel as its host runtime.
long_description.why_it_exists: Ensures consistent authentication and authorization discipline across all Grid nodes. Prevents scattered auth implementations and guarantees uniform identity checks regardless of entrypoint.
long_description.primary_audience: Service owners building HTTP APIs, gateways, and any Grid node requiring request authentication.
value_props: JWT Bearer token validation with Vault-backed signing keys
Deterministic policy evaluation (local, side-effect free, auditable)
Fail-fast startup validation of required secrets
Deep Kernel integration for telemetry, lifecycle, and context propagation
long_description.roadmap_focus: Passkeys and MFA support. Federated sign-ins and device trust. Pulse integration for session observability.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel (required) for GridContext, telemetry, and lifecycle. Consumes Vault (required) for signing keys and configuration. Exposes auth contracts consumed by Web.Rest and service nodes.
long_description.demo_path: Validate a JWT token and evaluate a policy with full telemetry tracing.

long_description.overview: Provider-agnostic authentication and authorization contracts for the Grid. Defines identity primitives and policy boundaries without coupling to any specific web framework.
long_description.why_it_exists: Prevents framework lock-in and keeps identity policy consistent across nodes, runtimes, and entrypoints.
long_description.primary_audience: Node and service developers consuming Auth contracts, plus maintainers implementing auth providers.
value_props: Stable auth contract surface across the Grid
Clear policy boundaries and decision modeling
Enables multiple implementations without consumer changes
long_description.roadmap_focus: Harden policy evaluation and decision auditing shapes.
long_description.grid_relationship: Defines the contracts consumed by Web.Rest and implemented by Auth providers.
long_description.demo_path: Use contracts to validate an identity and evaluate a policy without any ASP.NET runtime dependency.

long_description.overview: ASP.NET Core adapter that integrates HoneyDrunk.Auth contracts into the web pipeline for request authentication and policy enforcement.
long_description.why_it_exists: Most nodes expose HTTP surfaces early. This module provides first-class integration with ASP.NET Core so services can adopt Auth with minimal wiring.
long_description.primary_audience: Service owners building HTTP APIs and gateways.
value_props: Plugs Auth contracts into ASP.NET Core middleware and DI
Standardized policy enforcement patterns across services
Consistent identity evaluation and failure shaping
long_description.roadmap_focus: Expanded scheme support and richer audit hooks for policy decisions.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Auth adapter slot for ASP.NET Core entrypoints; consumed by Web.Rest and service nodes.
long_description.demo_path: Protect an endpoint, evaluate a policy, and emit a consistent authorization decision result.

long_description.overview: In-process message broker for testing Transport applications. Implements the full Transport abstraction (ITransportPublisher, ITransportConsumer) with Channel-based observable queues. Runs the exact same middleware pipeline, handler resolution, and context propagation logic as production transports—not a mock, but a real transport implementation.
long_description.why_it_exists: Developers need deterministic, fast integration tests that exercise the complete pipeline without external infrastructure.
long_description.primary_audience: Service developers writing integration tests for handlers and middleware.
value_props: Zero infrastructure—no Azure, no Docker, no external services
Full pipeline execution with middleware and handler invocation
Observable Channel-based queues for test verification
Deterministic execution with no timing issues or flaky tests
long_description.monetization_signal: Open source utility for dev/test only.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Fault injection toggles (delay, drop, duplicate) to harden handlers.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Transport adapter slot; used by Testing harnesses.
long_description.demo_path: Publish a message in-memory, observe full pipeline execution, assert handler outcomes.
long_description.signal_quote: Test like production, fail like development.

long_description.overview: Azure Storage Queue adapter for HoneyDrunk.Transport. A cost-effective (~10x cheaper than Service Bus), high-volume messaging solution for simple point-to-point queue scenarios. Features automatic poison queue handling, exponential backoff polling, and two-level concurrency control.
long_description.why_it_exists: High-volume workloads need durable queues at low cost without the complexity of full-fledged brokers.
long_description.primary_audience: Teams deploying on Azure with simple FIFO/background jobs requiring millions of messages per day.
value_props: Exponential backoff for empty queue polling optimization
long_description.roadmap_focus: Batch receive optimization and visibility timeout tuning.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Transport adapter slot; emits telemetry into Pulse.
long_description.demo_path: Publish messages to Storage Queue, observe dequeue/handle lifecycle via Pulse traces.
long_description.signal_quote: High volume, low cost, simple semantics.

long_description.overview: Azure Service Bus adapter for enterprise messaging scenarios. Supports topics/subscriptions (pub/sub), sessions (ordered processing), transactional receive (PeekLock), duplicate detection, and blob storage fallback for publish failures. Ideal for high-throughput, high-correctness workloads where ordering, transactions, and DLQ behavior matter.
long_description.why_it_exists: Mission-critical, event-driven services on Azure need high-throughput pub/sub with robust delivery semantics, ordering guarantees, and transactional behavior.
long_description.primary_audience: Production services requiring topics, sessions, transactions, and enterprise-grade message size support (up to 100MB).
value_props: Topics & subscriptions for pub/sub messaging patterns
Sessions for ordered message processing per session
Blob storage fallback for failed publishes
long_description.monetization_signal: Open core adapter; enterprise tuning guides later.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Advanced dead-letter strategies and per-subscription filters.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Transport adapter slot; integrates with Vault for secrets and Pulse for telemetry.
long_description.demo_path: Publish to a topic, handle via subscription, trace causality across services with Grid context.
long_description.signal_quote: Enterprise messaging with all the bells and whistles.

long_description.overview: Common telemetry contracts and helpers shared by adapters and services: span/metric naming, correlation, and severity conventions.
long_description.why_it_exists: Without shared contracts, telemetry becomes noisy and incomparable.
long_description.primary_audience: Developers instrumenting services and building sinks.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Expand semantic conventions for background jobs and brokers.
long_description.grid_relationship: Backs all Pulse telemetry modules and service instrumentation.
long_description.demo_path: Wrap a handler with shared helpers and see consistent spans in Pulse.

long_description.overview: OpenTelemetry wiring for the HoneyDrunk stack. OTLP exporters, resource detection, and auto-instrumentation presets.
long_description.why_it_exists: Standards-based telemetry reduces lock-in and unlocks rich tooling.
long_description.primary_audience: Service owners who want end-to-end traces and metrics quickly.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Sampling strategies and baggage propagation guides.
long_description.grid_relationship: Feeds Pulse. Works alongside vendor sinks like Sentry/PostHog.
long_description.demo_path: Enable preset → see service map and latency histograms in minutes.

long_description.overview: Sentry sink for error events and enriched traces with release and environment tagging.
long_description.why_it_exists: Tight error feedback loops demand actionable, enriched events.
long_description.primary_audience: Teams who prefer Sentry for error tracking.
value_props: Release + env tags out of the box
long_description.roadmap_focus: Performance spans alignment and sampling controls.
long_description.grid_relationship: One of Pulse’s optional sinks; coexists with OTLP export.
long_description.demo_path: Throw an exception and inspect the correlated event in Sentry.

long_description.overview: PostHog sink for product analytics and event capture aligned to the shared telemetry schema.
long_description.why_it_exists: Creators need behavior analytics without distorting engineering telemetry.
long_description.primary_audience: Product-facing services and web apps.
value_props: Clean event mapping from shared contracts
long_description.roadmap_focus: Sessionization helpers and privacy-friendly defaults.
long_description.grid_relationship: Optional Pulse sink for creator/product analytics.
long_description.demo_path: Emit a PageViewed event and analyze funnels in PostHog.

long_description.overview: Shared event contracts for the Pulse telemetry pipeline including PulseIngested and version definitions. Multi-targets netstandard2.0 and net10.0 for broad compatibility.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides the canonical event schema that Pulse producers and consumers agree on, enabling decoupled telemetry ingestion.
long_description.primary_audience: Services emitting or consuming Pulse telemetry events.
value_props: Canonical event contracts for telemetry pipeline
long_description.roadmap_focus: Expand event schema for new telemetry signals.
long_description.grid_relationship: Shared contracts consumed by Pulse Collector and downstream analytics services.
long_description.demo_path: Reference Pulse.Contracts to emit PulseIngested events.

long_description.overview: Loki sink for structured log forwarding via the shared telemetry ILogSink contract.
long_description.why_it_exists: Routes structured logs to Grafana Loki for centralized log aggregation without coupling services to a specific logging backend.
long_description.primary_audience: Teams using Grafana stack for log aggregation.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Batch optimization and tenant label injection.
long_description.grid_relationship: Optional Pulse sink for log aggregation.
long_description.demo_path: Register Loki sink and observe structured logs in Grafana.

long_description.overview: Tempo sink for distributed trace forwarding via the shared telemetry ITraceSink contract.
long_description.why_it_exists: Routes distributed traces to Grafana Tempo for trace storage and querying without coupling to a specific tracing backend.
long_description.primary_audience: Teams using Grafana stack for distributed tracing.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Sampling configuration and trace-to-log correlation.
long_description.grid_relationship: Optional Pulse sink for distributed trace storage.
long_description.demo_path: Register Tempo sink and visualize traces in Grafana.
long_description.signal_quote: Traces that tell the full story.

long_description.overview: Mimir sink for metrics forwarding via the shared telemetry IMetricsSink contract.
long_description.why_it_exists: Routes application metrics to Grafana Mimir for long-term metrics storage and querying without coupling to a specific metrics backend.
long_description.primary_audience: Teams using Grafana stack for metrics aggregation.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Custom metric dimensions and cardinality controls.
long_description.grid_relationship: Optional Pulse sink for metrics aggregation.
long_description.demo_path: Register Mimir sink and query metrics in Grafana.

long_description.overview: Azure Monitor sink for full-stack telemetry forwarding (traces, metrics, logs) via Azure Application Insights and the shared telemetry sink contracts.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides Azure-native observability for teams deploying to Azure, routing all telemetry signals through Application Insights without custom plumbing.
long_description.primary_audience: Teams deploying to Azure who want native monitoring integration.
value_props: Full-stack Azure Monitor integration (traces, metrics, logs)
Connection string and managed identity support
Works alongside other sinks without interference
long_description.roadmap_focus: Live metrics stream and profiler integration.
long_description.grid_relationship: Optional Pulse sink for Azure-native observability.
long_description.demo_path: Register Azure Monitor sink and observe telemetry in Application Insights.

long_description.overview: Deployable collector service that receives OTLP traces, metrics, and logs plus custom analytics and error events. Acts as the central ingestion point for all Pulse telemetry in the Grid.
long_description.why_it_exists: Centralizes telemetry ingestion with Grid-aware context enrichment, providing a single entry point for all observability data before routing to configured sinks.
long_description.primary_audience: Platform operators deploying the Pulse telemetry pipeline.
value_props: OTLP-native trace, metric, and log ingestion
Custom analytics and error event endpoints
long_description.roadmap_focus: Sampling policies, rate limiting, and multi-tenant routing.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Transport for event delivery. Consumes Vault for secrets. Routes to configured sinks (Loki, Tempo, Mimir, Sentry, PostHog, Azure Monitor).
long_description.demo_path: Deploy Collector, point OTLP exporters at it, observe telemetry flowing to backends.

long_description.overview: Core secrets and configuration management library providing abstractions, caching, orchestration, telemetry, and Kernel lifecycle integration. This package contains no provider implementations—it defines the Vault contract, runtime behavior, and coordination layer. Providers supply the values; this core handles resilience, caching, and distributed tracing.
long_description.why_it_exists: A unified, Kernel-aware interface for secrets and configuration that all provider packages plug into.
long_description.primary_audience: Service developers and infrastructure maintainers needing consistent secret/config access patterns.
value_props: Defines ISecretStore, IConfigProvider, IVaultClient, ISecretProvider, IConfigSource
In-memory caching with TTL and sliding expiration
Retry and circuit breaker resilience policies
Grid context propagation for tracing and correlation
Kernel lifecycle integration (startup hooks, health, readiness)
Secure telemetry that never logs secret values
long_description.roadmap_focus: Pulse integration for anomaly detection, autonomous credential rotation.
long_description.grid_relationship: Foundation for all Vault providers and consumed by Auth, AgentKit, and most service nodes.
long_description.demo_path: Inject ISecretStore and retrieve a secret with full tracing and caching.
long_description.signal_quote: The contract that keeps secrets safe.

long_description.overview: Azure Key Vault provider with managed identity support and versioned secret retrieval aligned to Vault contracts.
long_description.why_it_exists: Production-grade secret management for Azure-hosted nodes.
long_description.primary_audience: Service owners deploying on Azure.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Add richer caching/refresh controls and operational diagnostics.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Vault provider slot for Azure deployments.
long_description.demo_path: Deploy a node to Azure and resolve secrets via Key Vault.
long_description.signal_quote: Keys where the cloud keeps them.

long_description.overview: AWS Secrets Manager provider offering retrieval and caching aligned to Vault contracts.
long_description.why_it_exists: First-class Vault provider support for AWS-hosted nodes.
long_description.primary_audience: Service owners deploying on AWS.
value_props: Keeps AWS concerns isolated to the provider
long_description.roadmap_focus: Expand diagnostics and rotation-friendly refresh patterns.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Vault provider slot for AWS deployments.
long_description.demo_path: Resolve secrets from AWS Secrets Manager through Vault contracts.

long_description.overview: Provider that sources secrets and configuration from the application configuration pipeline for bootstrap and controlled environments.
long_description.why_it_exists: Enables clean bootstrap and integration with existing configuration sources without bypassing Vault contracts.
long_description.primary_audience: Infrastructure maintainers and developers wiring node configuration.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Clarify best practices for production vs dev usage.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Vault provider slot via configuration sources.
long_description.demo_path: Run a node using config-backed secrets for controlled dev environments.

long_description.overview: In-memory provider for tests and local development. Provides deterministic secrets/config without external infrastructure.
long_description.why_it_exists: Fast, reliable test and dev loops without cloud services or files.
long_description.primary_audience: Test harnesses and developers running locally.
long_description.monetization_signal: Open source utility for dev/test only.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Add fault injection and validation helpers for test realism.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Vault provider slot for testing and local development.
long_description.demo_path: Run integration tests with Vault-backed secrets entirely in memory.

long_description.overview: File-based provider for local and dev scenarios. Loads secrets and configuration from files in a predictable structure for easy rotation and auditing.
long_description.why_it_exists: Fast local workflows without cloud dependencies.
long_description.primary_audience: Developers running nodes locally.
value_props: Works well for dev and small deployments
long_description.roadmap_focus: Improve ergonomics and validation of secret/config layouts.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements the Vault provider slot for local/dev usage.
long_description.demo_path: Run a node locally with Vault reading secrets from files.

long_description.overview: Location verification with radius/shape geofences, dwell-time checks, and spoofing heuristics.
long_description.why_it_exists: Prevents fake completions and anchors IRL Gigs to real places.
long_description.primary_audience: All IRL Gig categories.
long_description.roadmap_focus: BLE/beacon support; indoor map hints.
long_description.grid_relationship: Integrates with POI provider; emits verification events to HiveGigs API and HiveXP.
long_description.demo_path: Enter fence → satisfy dwell → emit verified completion.

long_description.overview: Photo/video evidence checker using object detection/OCR prompts tied to Gig templates.
long_description.why_it_exists: Adds visual proof for challenges that need more than a GPS ping.
long_description.primary_audience: Museums, landmarks, scavenger tasks.
long_description.monetization_signal: Quality gate for premium creator payouts.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Few-shot tuning per template family.
long_description.grid_relationship: Stores artifacts in Vault-backed storage; emits pass/fail to HiveGigs and HiveXP.
long_description.demo_path: Upload artifact → detect target → verify.

long_description.overview: OpenStreetMap-backed POI adapter with filters for parks, museums, and cultural venues.
long_description.why_it_exists: Gives agents structured seeds for Gig generation.
long_description.primary_audience: Agent writers and curators.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Hybrid enrichment with Wikipedia/Wikidata.
long_description.grid_relationship: Feeds Agents.Writer; shares cache with Geo verifier.
long_description.demo_path: Query city → return POIs → generate candidate Gigs.

long_description.overview: Creator earnings and sponsor payments via Stripe Connect with split rules per Gig.
long_description.why_it_exists: Turns creation into income, safely and at scale.
long_description.primary_audience: Quest authors, partners, and Market ops.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Holdbacks, clawbacks, tax document automation.
long_description.grid_relationship: Uses Vault for secrets; emits financial events to Meta/MarketCore.
long_description.demo_path: Complete Gig → credit creator → payout batch.

long_description.overview: Real-time alerts for nearby Gigs, expiring missions, and creator earnings.
long_description.why_it_exists: Keeps engagement loops tight and timely.
long_description.primary_audience: Players and creators.
long_description.roadmap_focus: LLM-personalized mission recommendations.
long_description.grid_relationship: Push via Transport; web/mobile via SignalR or FCM/APNs bridge.
long_description.demo_path: Enter zone → receive mission ping → accept.

long_description.overview: Standardized ingestion of XP events from Nodes via Transport envelopes.
long_description.why_it_exists: Prevents bespoke XP plumbing per product.
long_description.primary_audience: Any Node emitting XP (Gigs, HoneyHub, HoneyPlay).
long_description.monetization_signal: Internal backbone for all Market-facing XP loops.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Bulk ingest and replay.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Transport; validates against Core schemas; forwards to Ledger.
long_description.demo_path: Emit event → validate → ledger write.

long_description.overview: Auditable XP transaction store with category partitions and rollups.
long_description.why_it_exists: Single source of truth for progression.
long_description.primary_audience: XP consumers and analytics.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Temporal tables; columnstore aggregates.
long_description.grid_relationship: Writes to SQL via Core data conventions; emits change feed to Analytics.
long_description.demo_path: Insert XP → query level/rollup → verify audit.

long_description.overview: Exponential level curve with configurable base and scaling factors.
long_description.why_it_exists: Gives consistent difficulty ramp across products.
long_description.primary_audience: Designers setting progression pace.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Hybrid curves (exp + plateau).
long_description.grid_relationship: Plug-in to Engine; used by Leaderboards/Profiles.
long_description.demo_path: Set curve → simulate 1–50 → export thresholds.

long_description.overview: Default XP categories (Creation, Discovery, Social, Learning) with weights and display strings.
long_description.why_it_exists: Shared language of effort across Nodes.
long_description.primary_audience: All XP emitters/consumers.
long_description.monetization_signal: N/A; improves UX consistency for Market products.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Sector-specific packs.
long_description.grid_relationship: Served by API; cached by clients; logged in Ledger.
long_description.demo_path: Fetch pack → emit events by category → show profile rollups.

long_description.overview: Sorted-set leaderboards by category, region, and time window.
long_description.why_it_exists: Fast public rankings without hitting the ledger hot path.
long_description.primary_audience: Public apps and Market surfaces.
long_description.monetization_signal: Premium board filters and seasonal events.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Anti-cheat outlier dampening.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes events from Ledger; exposes cached reads to apps.
long_description.demo_path: Emit XP → board update → query top N.

long_description.overview: Rule-based achievements over XP events and category milestones.
long_description.why_it_exists: Adds durable goals beyond raw levels.
long_description.primary_audience: All consumer apps.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Rule DSL with simulator.
long_description.grid_relationship: Reads Ledger; writes badge events; surfaces to Leaderboards/Profiles.
long_description.demo_path: Define rule → ingest events → award badge.

long_description.overview: Batch and streaming exports of XP events and rollups to the analytics warehouse.
long_description.why_it_exists: Lets Meta/Market surfaces run deep analysis without hammering OLTP.
long_description.primary_audience: Ops, analytics, and growth.
long_description.monetization_signal: Enables premium analytics for Market products.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Auto-generated BI datasets.
long_description.grid_relationship: Reads from Ledger; writes to warehouse; cataloged in Meta.
long_description.demo_path: Replay last 24h → build dashboard.

long_description.overview: Pure contracts and constants for HoneyDrunk REST services. Provides standardized response envelopes (ApiResult, ApiErrorResponse), pagination contracts (PageRequest, PageResult), error codes, header constants, and telemetry tags.
long_description.why_it_exists: Enables consistent API responses across all services without coupling to ASP.NET Core or any runtime framework. Zero NuGet dependencies.
long_description.primary_audience: All REST service developers and consumers needing type-safe API contracts.
value_props: Consistent error codes and header constants
long_description.monetization_signal: Open source foundation for REST consistency.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Expand response metadata, add batch result contracts.
long_description.grid_relationship: Foundation for HoneyDrunk.Web.Rest.AspNetCore; consumed by any service returning REST responses.
long_description.demo_path: Reference Abstractions → return ApiResult<T> from any .NET project.

long_description.overview: ASP.NET Core integration for HoneyDrunk REST conventions. Provides middleware for correlation propagation, exception mapping, and request logging. Includes MVC filters for model validation, minimal API endpoint conventions, and JSON serialization defaults.
long_description.why_it_exists: Ensures consistent API behavior (correlation IDs, error responses, structured logging) across all ASP.NET Core services with minimal configuration.
long_description.primary_audience: ASP.NET Core service developers building REST APIs.
long_description.roadmap_focus: OpenAPI enrichment, rate limiting hooks, response compression conventions.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumes Kernel.Abstractions for correlation context and Auth.AspNetCore for auth shaping. References Transport.Abstractions for envelope mapping only.
long_description.demo_path: Call AddRest() → UseRest() → all responses follow conventions automatically.

long_description.overview: Contracts-only package defining the notification abstractions: INotificationSender, INotificationGateway, INotificationPolicy, ITemplateRenderer, and all envelope/outcome types. Zero runtime dependencies.
long_description.why_it_exists: Separates notification contracts from implementation, enabling provider packages and consumers to depend only on abstractions.
long_description.primary_audience: Service developers sending notifications and notification provider implementors.
value_props: Policy pipeline for rate limiting and routing
Template rendering contracts for rich content
long_description.roadmap_focus: Push notification channel, webhook delivery contracts.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumed by all Notify provider packages and by services that send notifications.
long_description.demo_path: Reference Notify.Abstractions to build a notification request without coupling to a provider.

long_description.overview: Core runtime implementing NotificationGateway, NotificationDispatcher, sender resolution, template rendering (file-based with token replacement), idempotency, and retry with exponential backoff.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides the orchestration layer that routes notification requests through policies, resolves senders by channel, renders templates, and handles retries with idempotency.
long_description.primary_audience: Service developers integrating notification dispatch into applications.
value_props: File-based template rendering with model flattening
long_description.roadmap_focus: Batch dispatch, priority queuing, and Pulse telemetry hooks.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements Notify.Abstractions contracts. Extends Kernel for GridContext and lifecycle hooks.
long_description.demo_path: Wire up NotificationGateway → send an email → observe dispatch with retry and idempotency.

long_description.overview: ASP.NET Core hosting integration providing AddHoneyDrunkNotify() service registration, health contributors, and configuration binding for retry, policy, and template options.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides the DI wiring and health monitoring for Notify in ASP.NET Core hosts, following the same AddHoneyDrunk*() pattern as other Grid nodes.
long_description.primary_audience: ASP.NET Core service developers adding notification capabilities.
value_props: Health contributor for notification system status
Configuration binding for retry, policy, and template options
long_description.roadmap_focus: Management endpoints for notification queue inspection.
long_description.grid_relationship: Wires Notify core and providers into ASP.NET Core DI. Integrates with Kernel health checks.
long_description.demo_path: Call AddHoneyDrunkNotify() → health endpoint reports notification system status.

long_description.overview: SMTP email provider implementing INotificationSender for the Email channel. Configurable via SmtpOptions for host, port, credentials, and TLS.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides SMTP-based email delivery for environments using traditional mail servers or SMTP relay services.
long_description.primary_audience: Services sending email via SMTP.
value_props: Standard SMTP delivery with TLS support
long_description.roadmap_focus: Connection pooling and health monitoring.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements Notify.Abstractions INotificationSender for Email channel.
long_description.demo_path: Register SMTP provider → send an email notification.

long_description.overview: Resend email provider implementing INotificationSender for the Email channel using the Resend API for modern transactional email delivery.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides API-based email delivery via Resend for teams preferring a developer-friendly transactional email service over traditional SMTP.
long_description.primary_audience: Services sending transactional email via Resend.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Delivery webhooks and bounce tracking integration.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements Notify.Abstractions INotificationSender for Email channel.
long_description.demo_path: Register Resend provider → send a transactional email.

long_description.overview: Twilio SMS provider implementing INotificationSender for the SMS channel. Configurable via TwilioOptions for account SID, auth token, and sender number.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides SMS delivery via Twilio for transactional notifications like verification codes, alerts, and status updates.
long_description.primary_audience: Services sending SMS notifications via Twilio.
value_props: Twilio SMS delivery with TwilioOptions configuration
long_description.roadmap_focus: Delivery status webhooks and number verification.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements Notify.Abstractions INotificationSender for SMS channel.
long_description.demo_path: Register Twilio provider → send an SMS notification.

long_description.overview: Queue abstractions defining INotificationQueue and IDeadLetterInspector for async notification processing with dead-letter inspection support.
long_description.why_it_exists: Separates queue contracts from implementation, enabling InMemory and Azure Storage queue providers to be swapped without changing consumer code.
long_description.primary_audience: Notification queue provider implementors and services using async dispatch.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Priority queuing and delay scheduling contracts.
long_description.grid_relationship: Consumed by queue provider packages (InMemory, AzureStorage) and by Notify.Worker.
long_description.demo_path: Reference Queue.Abstractions to enqueue notifications for background processing.
long_description.signal_quote: Queue the intent, dispatch at will.

long_description.overview: In-memory queue implementation for development and testing scenarios. Implements INotificationQueue with channel-based async processing.
long_description.why_it_exists: Enables fast local development and integration testing of notification workflows without external queue infrastructure.
long_description.primary_audience: Developers testing notification flows locally.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Test assertion helpers for queue inspection.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements Queue.Abstractions for local development and testing.
long_description.demo_path: Register InMemory queue → enqueue and process notifications locally.

long_description.overview: Azure Storage Queue implementation for production notification queuing. Implements INotificationQueue with configurable visibility timeout and dead-letter support.
long_description.why_it_exists: Provides durable, low-cost queue infrastructure for production notification processing using Azure Storage Queues.
long_description.primary_audience: Services deploying notification processing on Azure.
long_description.roadmap_focus: Batch dequeue optimization and managed identity support.
long_description.grid_relationship: Implements Queue.Abstractions for production queue scenarios on Azure.
long_description.demo_path: Register Azure Storage queue → deploy notification processing with durable queuing.
